title: Armed Ambush Kills Unidentified Man in Ejido Balbuena, Navolato; ~100 Rifle Shell Casings Recovered
detail: An unidentified male — approximately 40 years old, thin build, wearing black jeans and a blue shirt — was shot and killed in the early morning of Sunday, April 26, 2026 in Ejido Balbuena, in the municipality of Navolato, Sinaloa. The attack occurred after 1:00 AM when unknown gunmen aboard at least two vehicles intercepted the victim just meters from his own residence. The man attempted to flee on foot but was struck by multiple rifle rounds and died at the scene. A nearby home sustained significant bullet damage from the sustained assault. Investigators from the State Attorney General's Office (FGE) processed the crime scene and recovered approximately 100 spent rifle shell casings — an unusually high count suggesting a prolonged or multi-shooter coordinated attack. The Navolato municipality borders Culiacán directly to the west and has seen persistent cartel activity as factions contest control of coastal smuggling corridors leading to the Pacific port of Altata. The killing comes one day after nine homicides across Sinaloa on April 25, continuing the elevated baseline violence pattern in the state despite year-over-year improvements in monthly homicide totals.

media.caption: Armed attack in Ejido Balbuena, Navolato at 1 AM — 1 killed, house riddled with bullets, ~100 shell casings recovered by FGE investigators
